mnemonics: SET your RULES: Sequential Covering Evaluates Training examples, Repeatedly Updates and Learns Entailed Sentences
keyPoints: Sequential covering algorithms learn one rule at a time, remove the covered examples, and repeat
Rules are typically learned in a greedy fashion, maximizing coverage of positive examples
Rule sets can be post-pruned to improve generalization and avoid overfitting
commonMistakes: Failing to remove covered examples after learning each rule, leading to redundant rules
Not considering the order of rules, which is crucial as the first matching rule fires
Confusing rule learning with decision tree learning, where rules are learned independently rather than in a hierarchical structure
